title: Preserve semantics in every transformation
description: Treat each optimization or lowering as a behavior-preserving rewrite and prove it on the full input domain, not one example.
body: Use when writing or reviewing any optimization or lowering pass. Do not assume a faster/smaller output is correct because it works on a sample, and do not ignore edge cases (overflow, division by zero, undefined behavior, NaN, aliasing). Actions: state the precondition under which the rewrite is legal, apply it only when that holds, and verify with differential testing of optimized vs unoptimized output across a corpus. Validation signals include matching results on a large program corpus and an IR verifier pass after the transform. Risk boundary: a miscompilation silently corrupts every program it touches and is extremely hard to trace from the symptom; treat any pass that cannot be shown semantics-preserving as blocking and escalate for review.
